jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams] - [All] Ow! Ow! Ow! - Ow! Ow! Hmm!
jokes.setup: Car crash during opening credits sequence
jokes.punchline: Family's synchronized pain reaction with Homer's signature exclamation
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer 'D'oh!' moment with family participation, economical and character-specific

jokes.text: Here's the mail, Dad. That'll be three dollars for on-couch delivery.
jokes.setup: Child delivering mail to parent in their own home
jokes.punchline: Charging for basic household service with absurd specificity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart character comedy - entrepreneurial greed applied to family life with precise pricing structure

jokes.text: This isn't real money. It's printed by the Montana Militia. It'll be real soon enough.
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to militia currency with ominous confidence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy mixing financial humor with anti-government extremism in perfectly casual delivery

jokes.text: I'm betting it's hunger and rage. Yeah, but at what ratio?
jokes.setup: Homer wondering what makes him tick
jokes.punchline: Children clinically analyzing their father's base motivations with scientific precision
jokes.explanation: Excellent character insight joke - kids treating Homer like a lab specimen with perfect comic timing

jokes.text: My way is easier.
jokes.setup: Homer not reading magazine quizzes properly
jokes.punchline: Defending laziness with matter-of-fact logic
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer logic - taking shortcuts and defending them with complete confidence

jokes.text: Ooh, quizmaster, yes. That would entertain me briefly.
jokes.punchline: Homer's honest admission about his short attention span
jokes.explanation: Self-aware Homer moment - acknowledging his own fleeting interests with unusual honesty

jokes.text: That makes you a... Frigid Frieda. I took off 30 points for all that crying you did.
jokes.setup: Homer giving Flanders a love quiz score
jokes.punchline: Cruel nickname plus penalizing grieving behavior
jokes.explanation: Dark Homer insensitivity - treating grief as a scoring penalty with casual cruelty

jokes.text: No way! When? Six months ago. You were at the funeral. You fell into the grave.
jokes.setup: Homer shocked by news of Maude's death
jokes.punchline: Homer was actually at the funeral but fell into the grave
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback comedy - Homer's obliviousness to major events despite being physically present in spectacular fashion

jokes.text: Oh, yeah. I saw a gopher. What a day.
jokes.punchline: Focusing on irrelevant detail from traumatic/solemn event
jokes.explanation: Peak Homer absurdity - finding joy in wildlife observation while literally in someone's grave. Perfect economy and character work.

jokes.text: Oh, the little rat-faced one. No, no, no. Nick. He's so good to his mother.
jokes.punchline: Dismissive description vs. maternal appreciation
jokes.explanation: Decent old man pop culture confusion with contrasting superficial vs. character-based preferences

jokes.text: According to this, you're both idiots. Hey, thanks. What do we owe you?
jokes.punchline: Grateful acceptance and offer to pay for the insult
jokes.explanation: Great subversion - treating devastating quiz results as valuable service worthy of payment

jokes.text: Homie, for God sakes, it's 2:00 a.m. Fine. I'll take the next quiz.
jokes.setup: Marge trying to stop Homer's late-night quiz obsession
jokes.punchline: Homer completely missing her point and continuing anyway
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer obliviousness - interpreting complaint as suggestion to continue behavior

jokes.text: Marge, do you think that counts honey-braised? I don't know! I'm trying to sleep!
jokes.setup: Homer taking longevity quiz about ribs at 2 AM
jokes.punchline: Obsessing over technicalities while Marge desperately wants sleep
jokes.explanation: Escalating marital comedy - Homer's food obsession overriding social awareness

jokes.text: So according to this, I'll live to be- 42?
jokes.punchline: Shockingly low number delivered with perfect timing pause
jokes.explanation: Well-structured reveal joke with perfect pause timing before devastating number

jokes.text: I won't even live to see my children die.
jokes.setup: Homer lamenting his short life expectancy
jokes.punchline: Perverse parental milestone - wanting to outlive your children
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark Homer logic - treating outliving your children as a normal parental goal. Incredibly quotable and twisted.

jokes.text: See? And these quizzes are never wrong, Marge. They're put together by the finest scientists in the magazine business.
jokes.punchline: Oxymoronic appeal to 'magazine scientists' authority
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer pseudo-logic - creating fake authority by combining 'scientists' with 'magazine business'

jokes.text: Can't sleep. Gonna die. Can't sleep. Gonna die.
jokes.punchline: Repetitive mantra reducing existential dread to simple rhyme
jokes.explanation: Effective anxiety comedy - reducing profound fear to childlike repetitive chant

jokes.text: I'm gonna kill you, Homer. You are so dead.
jokes.setup: TV interview with producer Robert Evans
jokes.punchline: TV personality threatening Homer specifically through the screen
jokes.explanation: Clever paranoia comedy - Homer's anxiety making TV seem personally threatening

jokes.text: Evans, you forgot Hollywood rule number one- kill Homer Simpson.
jokes.setup: Previous threat was interpreted as Homer's paranoia
jokes.punchline: TV actually is targeting Homer specifically as Hollywood priority
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - confirming Homer's paranoid delusions were actually correct all along

jokes.text: I'm not crazy. It's the TV that's crazy! Aren't you, TV?
jokes.punchline: Asking the TV to confirm his sanity claim
jokes.explanation: Good paranoia comedy - Homer seeking validation from the very source of his persecution

jokes.text: So tell me, are all you government inspectors so handsome and strapping?
jokes.punchline: Transparent attempt at seduction/bribery
jokes.explanation: Standard Burns corruption comedy - obvious flattery attempt with his typical inappropriate approach

jokes.text: You got to hide me. Death is after me.
jokes.punchline: Paranoid plea delivered at worst possible moment
jokes.explanation: Solid cringe comedy - Homer's mental breakdown creating maximum inconvenience

jokes.text: He's just a harmless maniac... here to remind us of the precious gift of sanity.
jokes.punchline: Spinning mental breakdown as inspirational workplace feature
jokes.explanation: Excellent Burns spin-mastery - reframing employee mental breakdown as motivational workplace amenity

jokes.text: That's it. That's it. A mother can't die. And I'm a mother. See?
jokes.punchline: Claiming motherhood status as death loophole
jokes.explanation: Brilliant desperate logic - Homer's gender confusion as mortality escape mechanism

jokes.text: Sometimes. But the guy I really hate is your father.
jokes.setup: Doctor asking if Bart hates his father
jokes.punchline: Redirecting hatred to doctor's father instead
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection - Bart deflecting therapy question with absurd personal attack on therapist

jokes.text: Because it's made of plastic. I see.
jokes.setup: Homer asking why his 'baby' isn't gaining weight
jokes.punchline: Obvious explanation delivered matter-of-factly, Homer accepts it
jokes.explanation: Solid absurdist comedy - Homer's delusion meeting clinical reality with mutual acceptance

jokes.text: Florida? But that's America's wang.
jokes.punchline: Crude anatomical description of state geography
jokes.explanation: Highly quotable geographical humor - crude but accurate observation about Florida's shape

jokes.text: My pockets hurt.
jokes.punchline: Completely nonsensical physical complaint
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurdist Homer complaint - creating impossible physical ailment with complete sincerity

jokes.text: Oh, no! We came during spring break!
jokes.punchline: Arriving during most chaotic possible time
jokes.explanation: Standard situational irony - seeking peace but finding chaos through bad timing

jokes.text: Take 'em off the glass! Take 'em off the glass!
jokes.punchline: Marge horrified by students pressing against car windows
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy inferred from Marge's horrified reaction to unseen spring break behavior

jokes.text: Party! Whoo-hoo!
jokes.setup: Homer was seeking rest and relaxation
jokes.punchline: Instantly embracing the chaos he was supposed to avoid
jokes.explanation: Predictable Homer character turn - abandoning medical advice for immediate gratification

jokes.text: Jealous of your boobs
jokes.punchline: Bizarre confession with no context
jokes.explanation: Weak random humor - relies on shock value without setup or character work

jokes.text: No! Not yet! I'm only 25!
jokes.punchline: Treating 25 as ancient age worth panicking about
jokes.explanation: Decent age anxiety comedy - youth culture's distorted perception of aging

jokes.text: Oh, no! It's a lost child! Don't worry, folks. I'll tackle him.
jokes.setup: Adult sees Homer dancing with young people
jokes.punchline: Mistaking middle-aged man for lost child requiring physical intervention
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup - Homer's childlike behavior causing literal confusion about his age

jokes.text: Do you have any hobo chunks we can throw to him?
jokes.setup: Tourists asking about feeding the alligator
jokes.punchline: Casually requesting human remains as animal treats
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark tourism humor - treating human remains as convenient gator snacks with disturbing casualness

jokes.text: Oh, he can crawl up through my toilet any day.
jokes.punchline: Sexual interest in reptilian home invasion
jokes.explanation: Disturbing sexual humor - combining attraction with dangerous toilet scenario

jokes.text: But this ain't no 40-ounce curb. So we're gonna need 40 gallons, y'all!
jokes.setup: Pouring 40-ounce beer for fallen homies tradition
jokes.punchline: Scaling up ceremony to ridiculous beach proportions
jokes.explanation: Good escalation comedy - taking street tradition and inflating it to absurd beach scale

jokes.text: All for Homer. Hey, who's that fool? Yo, let's waste that beeyotch!
jokes.setup: 40 gallons being poured in Homer's honor
jokes.punchline: Immediate hostility toward the supposed beneficiary
jokes.explanation: Good irony - honoring someone with expensive tribute then immediately wanting to attack them

jokes.text: No, sir. Please don't tell Mama.
jokes.setup: Sheriff scolding Joe C about his behavior
jokes.punchline: Tough rapper afraid of maternal disappointment
jokes.explanation: Decent character contrast - tough guy persona vs. maternal fear

jokes.text: Guess how many boobs I saw today, Marge? Fifteen!
jokes.setup: Homer reporting to Marge about his day
jokes.punchline: Proudly quantifying inappropriate behavior with specific count
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer inappropriateness - treating voyeurism as achievement worth sharing with wife

jokes.text: Doesn't he talk funny?
jokes.punchline: Lisa's innocent observation about regional speech patterns
jokes.explanation: Mild regional humor - child's perspective on dialect differences, fairly predictable

jokes.text: Dad, look out! - Look out for what - The giant gator - The giant- [Gasps, Screams]
jokes.punchline: Alligator attack interrupting his confident dismissal
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff structure with Homer's obliviousness leading to comeuppance

jokes.text: You killed Captain Jack. You in a heap of trouble, son.
jokes.punchline: Serious legal consequences for killing beloved local gator
jokes.explanation: Plot escalation point - Homer's vacation fun turning into serious legal trouble

jokes.text: Did you really have to handcuff the children? No, ma'am, I did not.
jokes.punchline: Sheriff admitting children's handcuffing was unnecessary choice
jokes.explanation: Good authority abuse comedy - sheriff's honest admission of unnecessary child restraint

jokes.text: You see, during spring break, the beer companies pay me to look the other way. The rest of the year, I'm a real hard-ass.
jokes.punchline: Casual corruption confession about seasonal law enforcement
jokes.explanation: Excellent corruption comedy - sheriff openly explaining his seasonal bribery system with complete casualness

jokes.text: Whoa! Looks like we got a teeny Houdini here. Well, don't you worry, darlin'. I got some baby cuffs in the station.
jokes.punchline: Sheriff having specialized infant restraint equipment
jokes.explanation: Great escalation - sheriff having baby-specific law enforcement equipment implies regular infant arrests

jokes.text: It worked! It's a miracle!
jokes.punchline: Treating basic car function as divine intervention
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer amazement at normal events - treating car starting as supernatural occurrence

jokes.text: Now why does that only happen when nobody's looking?
jokes.punchline: Character acknowledging the visual limitation of their world
jokes.explanation: Clever meta humor - character commenting on their animated medium's visual limitations

jokes.text: There's lots of good places! What about over there? No shade!
jokes.punchline: Homer prioritizing parking comfort over safety
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer priority comedy - treating life-threatening chase as parking inconvenience

jokes.text: Homer, no. You'll kill us all. Or die trying.
jokes.setup: Homer about to make dangerous driving decision
jokes.punchline: Homer accepting death as acceptable outcome
jokes.explanation: Good Homer determination comedy - treating family death as acceptable alternative to capture

jokes.text: The only thing we can do. Sit tight and try to get some sleep.
jokes.setup: Family trapped in precarious train situation
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual acceptance of deadly circumstance
jokes.explanation: Good Homer crisis management - treating mortal danger as minor inconvenience requiring rest

jokes.text: He said there's a procedure for dealing with events of this nature.
jokes.setup: Homer talking to engineer about their situation
jokes.punchline: Professional procedure is apparently explosive train destruction
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - 'procedure' implying safety but actually meaning destruction

jokes.text: Oh! Okay. Now am I the only one on fire?
jokes.punchline: Homer's practical concern about fire distribution
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer crisis response - treating being on fire as distribution equity issue rather than emergency

jokes.text: I love being married.
jokes.setup: Marge blaming Homer for their terrible situation
jokes.punchline: Homer finding joy in marital criticism
jokes.explanation: Good marriage comedy - Homer appreciating relationship even during harsh criticism

jokes.text: I just want to be a broom boy so bad.
jokes.punchline: Desperate enthusiasm for menial labor position
jokes.explanation: Decent desperation comedy - Homer's over-enthusiasm for basic janitorial work

jokes.text: I like your attitude. You're hired. How about you, missy? Do you want to be a mop girl? Not really, no. I like your honesty. You're hired.
jokes.punchline: Hiring people for opposite responses with same enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Good hiring absurdity - employer valuing contradictory qualities equally

jokes.text: And you two haven't said a word. I like that. You're hired.
jokes.punchline: Hiring people for complete silence
jokes.explanation: Escalation of hiring gag - finding positive in any behavior including none

jokes.text: But I raised eight young-uns, three chilluns and a baby here.
jokes.punchline: Confusing classification system for offspring
jokes.explanation: Mild dialect humor - Southern character's confusing child categorization system

jokes.text: You're insincere. I like that.
jokes.punchline: Continuing pattern of praising negative qualities
jokes.explanation: Callback to hiring gag - appreciating dishonesty as desirable employee trait

jokes.text: Well, I'm not one to judge. The way I see it, we're all sinners.
jokes.punchline: Casual moral relativism about serious crimes
jokes.explanation: Good Southern character philosophy - treating criminal fugitives as minor moral lapses

jokes.text: Stop that! Scat! Go on now! Just quit.
jokes.punchline: Treating person like annoying animal
jokes.explanation: Basic visual gag inferred - someone being shooed away like stray animal

jokes.text: Teacher says I'm whittling at a 1 0th grade level.
jokes.punchline: Academic achievement measurement for primitive skill
jokes.explanation: Good education satire - applying academic standards to basic craft skills

jokes.text: Someday, when Lisa and Bart get married, it'll all be theirs. Yuck! You mean when they marry other people. Okay, but I ain't payin' for two weddin's.
jokes.punchline: Implying sibling marriage, then cheapness about separate weddings
jokes.explanation: Good multi-layered comedy - incest implication followed by typical Homer cheapness about weddings

jokes.text: Hey, you're stealing my trailer! I like that.
jokes.setup: Sheriff taking trailer with family inside
jokes.punchline: Continuing pattern of appreciating negative behavior
jokes.explanation: Callback escalation - praising theft of her own property continues her positive attitude toward criminal behavior

jokes.text: Drunken hicks of the jury-
jokes.punchline: Immediately insulting the people who will decide his fate
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer self-sabotage - opening legal defense with jury insults

jokes.text: You know, you catch more flies with honey than with vinegar.
jokes.punchline: Homer offering management advice during punishment
jokes.explanation: Good situational irony - offering prisoner management tips while being whipped

jokes.text: No listenin'. You hear me? Uh, no.
jokes.punchline: Homer's logical literal response
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay - Homer's literal interpretation creating logical paradox for guard

jokes.text: What am I supposed to do?
jokes.setup: Being told to replace missing jazz pianist
jokes.punchline: Homer's reasonable confusion about impossible task
jokes.explanation: Basic setup for upcoming musical disaster - Homer's reasonable question about unreasonable demand

jokes.text: Restock the possum bar. We're almost out of claws.
jokes.punchline: Exotic regional cuisine requiring specific animal parts
jokes.explanation: Good regional stereotype with grotesque food service detail

jokes.text: Restock the possum bar. We're almost out of claws.
jokes.punchline: Grotesque Southern delicacy requiring specific anatomical parts
jokes.explanation: Excellent grotesque regional humor - possum claws as depleted party appetizer

jokes.text: Nah. Not on a civil servant's salary. It's near-sucker.
jokes.punchline: Knockoff fabric name based on budget constraints
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay - fabric quality joke based on public service financial limitations

jokes.text: You should see it in August after the horseflies been getting at it. Hoo-man!
jokes.punchline: Proudly describing insect-enhanced redneck development
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy - sheriff treating bug bites as desirable redneck enhancement

jokes.text: I might could switch you to dead animal pick up.
jokes.punchline: Offering roadkill collection as upgrade from tar digging
jokes.explanation: Prison work hierarchy with grim job options

jokes.text: Well, we're still welcome in North Dakota and Arizona. Arizona smells funny.
jokes.punchline: Rejecting option based on olfactory concerns
jokes.explanation: Mild geographic humor - family's limited options with arbitrary state preferences

jokes.text: I've always wanted to see Mount Rushmore. That's South Dakota.
jokes.setup: Homer's tourist enthusiasm for North Dakota
jokes.punchline: Geographic confusion about landmark location
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer geography ignorance - typical tourist knowledge failure
